title: centaurea kotschyi subsp. floccosa
intro: Centaurea kotschyi subsp. floccosa, also known as Kotschy's knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing and pain relief. The roots of Centaurea kotschyi subsp. floccosa can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for use in herbal medicine, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the specific preparation method.
